adds.rationale: European Commission President von der Leyen unveiled an 800B euro ReArm Europe plan on March 4, 2025 after Trump paused Ukraine aid, marking a structural geopolitical wave inflection; EUAD is the cleanest US-listed pure-play European aerospace and defense ETF (launched Oct 22, 2024) and broadens the watchlist's geopolitical-defense exposure beyond US primes ITA and NOC.

news_evidence.summary: Von der Leyen announces ReArm Europe plan to mobilize up to 800B euros in defense spending over four years; Europe enters an 'era of rearmament' after Trump pauses Ukraine aid.
Select STOXX Europe Aerospace & Defense ETF (EUAD) launched October 22, 2024 as the first US-listed pure-play European A&D ETF, capturing names like Rheinmetall, BAE, Leonardo, and Airbus.

removes.rationale: DeepSeek R1 (Jan 27, 2025) crystallized a 'cheap inference' narrative that hits second-tier AI-accelerator names hardest, and AMD's MI300 ramp has been visibly disappointing relative to NVDA share gains and Street expectations; the watchlist already has NVDA, AVGO, TSM, and ASML covering AI-semis, and the AI bucket is overstacked at 7 tickers versus the 4-per-bucket curator guideline.

news_evidence.summary: Nvidia falls 17% on Jan 27, 2025 as DeepSeek's R1 model triggers a global tech selloff, with AMD and other AI-semi names dragged down on the same 'cheaper AI compute' fear.
AMD CEO flags data center sales slowdown and heightened NVIDIA competition; MI300 traction has disappointed and AI data-center revenue is showing pressure from export restrictions on MI308 to China.

rationale_overall: Watchlist stays at the 24-ticker cap with one pair: add EUAD to capture the March 4 ReArm Europe inflection (the largest geopolitical-defense policy shift since the Ukraine invasion), and remove AMD whose competitive position in AI accelerators weakened further after the DeepSeek shock while the watchlist already carries four AI-semi names. Net thematic effect: geopolitical-defense exposure broadens from US-only (ITA, NOC) to include European primes via EUAD, while the still-crowded AI bucket loses its weakest semi and rebalances slightly toward higher-conviction names. Nuclear (4), quantum (2), rockets (1), and robotics (1) buckets unchanged; AI infra (VRT, GEV) and nuclear (CEG, VST) continue to fit the Stargate $500B AI-power tailwind announced Jan 21.
